dismissalText.commentary: Ryan Rickelton c Patidar b Kumar 2 (4b 0x4 0x6 4m) SR: 50

dismissalComment.html: This time it is straight to Patidar. What a start for RCB! Good length ball on off stump, Rickelton wants to loft this over mid-off, but doesn't get it anywhere near the middle. The ball floats to Patidar, who takes the simplest of catches at mid-off

dismissalText.commentary: Rohit Sharma c †Sharma b Kumar 22 (10b 2x4 2x6 14m) SR: 220

dismissalComment.html: Bhuvi has another. The swing merchant has been taking us on a nostalgia trip all season and here he is at it again. Gets rid of Rohit. A knuckle ball from Bhuvi, as he dangles it on a length well outside off. Rohit stretches forward, pushes at it but can only get a thick outside edge. Jitesh dives to his right and takes a lovely catch. Kohli is pumping his fists. RCB are on top

dismissalText.commentary: Naman Dhir b Rasikh Salam 47 (32b 5x4 2x6 60m) SR: 146.87

dismissalComment.html: Knocks him over! Two boundaries and a wicket. Cross-seam length ball outside off stays low and skids through as Dhir swipes across the line and knocks off the leg stump. That's a big breakthrough just as MI got a partnership going

dismissalText.commentary: Suryakumar Yadav c Kohli b Kumar 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 1m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: But he won't be getting any this game. My word! SKY has fallen for a first-ball duck. A typical Bhuvi dismissal this. Lands the ball on a length just outside off, shaping the ball away from SKY late. He pushes at it, gets a thick outside edge, with the ball travelling to Kohli's right at slip. He takes a good catch and RCB are buzzing. Bhuvi is on fire; MI are in disarray.

dismissalText.commentary: Tilak Varma b Kumar 57 (42b 3x4 2x6 79m) SR: 135.71

dismissalComment.html: Oh, he's played that onto his stumps! Bhuvi has a fourth! Bhuvneshwar was looking for a seam-up yorker, and Tilak went across to scoop. But he doesn't get under the ball and gets an inside edge that deflects the ball onto the stumps. Bhuvneshwar ends his spell with a well deserved four-for

dismissalText.commentary: Will Jacks c Bethell b Shepherd 10 (10b 1x4 0x6 14m) SR: 100

dismissalComment.html: Easy pickings for Bethell and Jacks is walking back to the dugout. On a length outside off and Jacks looks to whip it away. Doesn't come off the middle at all and the ball goes high towards deep midwicket. Bethell runs in, settles under it, and takes an easy catch. Mumbai Indians lose a fifth

dismissalText.commentary: Krunal Pandya c Tilak Varma b Ghazanfar 73 (46b 4x4 5x6 79m) SR: 158.69

dismissalComment.html: Tilak now does the relay catch all by himself! Full outside off and Krunal hits it flat to long-on's left. Tilak runs across, takes the catch, throws the ball up before crossing the rope and then hops back over to complete the catch. He grabbed it at first go a fair bit away from his body, and that's pretty great fielding. He makes up for his little gaffe from earlier in the over. Although, the 12 runs scored in between could still prove crucial. RCB, however, have lost their set batter. Krunal walks off after a spirited 73, fighting not just the opposition but also cramps
